terms.definition: A reusable, declarative piece of test setup (and teardown) that pytest injects into tests by matching argument names to `@pytest.fixture` functions.
Special pytest file containing fixtures auto-discoverable by every test in the same directory tree, no import needed.
How often a fixture is re-created: `function` (default, per test), `class`, `module`, `session` (once per pytest run).
Built-in pytest fixture providing a unique per-test temp directory (pathlib.Path), automatically cleaned up.
Built-in pytest fixture for temporarily setting attributes, env vars or dict items for the duration of a single test; auto-reverted.
A test double that records how it's called and returns configured values; in Python, `unittest.mock.MagicMock`.
A test double that returns canned values without asserting on calls; subset of what a Mock can do.
A working alternative implementation suitable for tests but not production (e.g. in-memory SQLite, in-memory FAISS index).
A wrapper around a real object that records every call made to it, often used to verify behaviour while still using the real implementation.
`unittest.mock.patch` — temporarily replaces a named attribute (`module.Name`) with a mock; used as decorator or context manager.
Design pattern where collaborators are passed into a unit (constructor/parameter) rather than created internally; makes mocking straightforward.
